description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a sloped frame. The panels are supported by a visible truss structure, likely made of metal, which elevates them above a base platform. The base includes patterned surfaces and several distinct modules, including a blue section that may represent an access or service area and a red section that could house electrical components. The structure appears to be designed for ground installation, with the panels tilted to optimize sunlight capture. The overall design suggests a robust, weather-resistant build suitable for urban or semi-urban environments.
description: A large, sloped solar panel array mounted on a metal truss above a patterned base with distinct blue and red modules, designed for ground installation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sloped solar panel surface and a blue module at the base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the truss support structure, the full length of the panel array, and both blue and red modules at the base.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the patterned base and a prominent red module, with the panel array visible above.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the truss structure and the base platform, with the blue module on the right.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal truss frame, likely aluminum or steel
attribute_array_configuration: single row of large panels forming a continuous rectangular array
